mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and falling consumer spending, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, invalidating the earnings compression channel.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized demand recovery across consumer and capital goods sectors, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60d returns with a statistically significant coefficient (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production reflects weakening manufacturing output and supply chain bottlenecks, which triggers short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines do not precede negative 5d SPX returns during late-cycle phases, with out-of-sample directional accuracy below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces anticipatory monetary tightening, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Inflation spikes above the threshold consistently coincide with positive 20d SPX returns, invalidating the margin compression channel.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields a negative or zero alpha over 60 days in walk-forward testing across multiple cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth directly increases corporate revenue expectations and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 revisions show no statistically significant correlation with 5-day forward SPX returns across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production breaking above its long-term trend signals capacity utilization recovery and improved operational leverage for industrial firms, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO trend crossings fail to predict positive 20d returns with an information coefficient greater than 0.05 over a 5-year sample.
